bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility and shows no concession pattern yet, so I will counter with my strongest values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid while making only a minimal concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer, which is very low utility for me and shows no concession yet, so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while offering flexibility on low-weight issueE.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid repeating my previous offer. The opponent has held firm on all valueE offers, so I concede the low-weight issueE to valueE while preserving my strongest preferences on higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not shown any real concession, repeating the same all-valueE offer each time. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while still preserving my most important issues, I am conceding only on lower-weight issueD and issueE toward valueE while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.08) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueE package worth little to me. I will keep my high-weight issues A, B, and C at my best values, while offering flexibility on lower-weight issues, especially issueE, and varying issueD to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.77, conceding mainly on the lower-weight issues D and E while holding firm on my highest-value choices for issues A, B, and C. Since it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility all-valueE offer without concession, there is no reason to make a major concession yet. This bid also avoids repeating my immediately previous bid while still moving toward the opponent’s apparent preference for valueE on less important issues.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on my lower-weight issues. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer without concession, so I am not treating it as a reason to give up high-weight issues yet; I will avoid exactly repeating my last bid by varying a low-importance issue.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.77 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer without conceding, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueE bid. I will continue holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C, while offering concessions mainly on the lower-weight issues D and E; this also avoids repeating my immediately previous bid exactly.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.77 by holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C while conceding lower-weight issues D and E toward the opponent’s repeated valueE pattern. Since it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has not conceded at all, I should not make major concessions yet, but I will avoid exactly repeating my previous bid.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high-utility bid while leaving concessions mainly on low-weight issues. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer with no concession, so I am holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C while varying my previous bid slightly to avoid exact repetition.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making a more meaningful concession than my earlier low-issue-only moves. The opponent has held firm on all valueE with no messages, so at t=0.22 I should not capitulate but should test whether conceding issueB somewhat, while holding my highest-value issueA and issueC positions, improves acceptance chances.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low 0.70s because it is early-mid negotiation time and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering all valueE. Since their behavior suggests they may strongly prefer valueE, I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueA and issueC while conceding lower-weight issueD and issueE to valueE and offering a moderate concession on issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, since it is only t=0.26 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueE bid. I will keep my highest-weight priorities on issueA and issueC, while making a modest additional concession on issueB and leaving lower-weight issueD/issueE at valueE to signal flexibility without over-conceding early.

I am targeting a utility around 0.704, keeping my highest-weight issues issueA and issueC at their top values while conceding lower-weight issueD and issueE to valueE. The opponent has held completely firm on all-valueE offers with no concession, but at t=0.28 it is still too early to drop close to their bid, so I make a moderate concession without sacrificing core value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, since we are only at t=0.30 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the same all-valueE bid. I am keeping my highest-weight issues issueA and issueC at top values, while conceding on lower-weight issueD and issueE and making a moderate concession on issueB to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a strong mid-utility offer while keeping my highest-weight issues, especially issueA and issueC, near my preferred values. The opponent has held completely firm on all valueE offers, so at t=0.32 I will continue conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and some issueB flexibility, but not collapse to their repeated low-utility bid yet.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.65, conceding mainly on lower-to-moderate priority issues while holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueC preferences. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer without concession, so at t=0.34 I will make a measured concession but not collapse to their position this early.

I am targeting a utility around 0.61 by holding firm on my highest-value issues issueA and issueC while conceding issueB further and keeping low-weight issueD/issueE at the opponent-signaled valueE. Since time is still only 0.36, I should not collapse to the opponent’s all-valueE bid, especially because they have shown no concession at all and sent no messages.

I am targeting about 0.65 utility by holding firm on my highest-value issues, especially issueA and issueC, while conceding on lower-weight issues and partially on issueB. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s repeated all-valueE position, and their offers show no concession or useful signal beyond a strong preference for valueE. With no messages to consider, I will avoid repeating my exact last bid while still offering meaningful concessions.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.61: enough concession to test movement, but still protecting my most important issues. The opponent has held completely firm on all valueE offers, so I am conceding toward valueE on lower/less critical issues while holding issueA and issueC, which are high-value for me.

At t=0.42 the opponent has held firm on the all-valueE bid, which is only about 0.25 utility for me, so I should not collapse to it yet. I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.51 by keeping my strongest issueC at valueB while conceding further on lower/medium issues and moving issueA from my ideal valueA to valueB as a compromise. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and all observed offers use valid domain values.

At t=0.44 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.55 rather than holding only near-maximum bids. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueE with no concessions and no messages, so the only signal is that they likely prefer valueE broadly; I concede low-weight issueD and issueE to valueE while protecting issueC and only partly conceding issueA/issueB.

At t=0.46 we are in the mid-game, so I am conceding further but still protecting my highest-value issues, especially issueA and issueC. The opponent has mostly held firm on valueE across issues, with only a small recent move on issueD, so I will vary my prior bid and concede issueD toward their latest signal while keeping a reasonable utility target above their offers to me.

At t=0.48 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a moderate-utility bid rather than collapsing to the opponent’s repeated all-valueE position. The opponent has mostly held firm, with only a small change on issueD, so I will concede on lower-priority/previously contested issues while holding stronger positions on issueA and especially issueC. This bid improves compatibility with their apparent preference for valueE on several issues while keeping my utility above a minimal acceptance level.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.49, conceding heavily on lower-value issues while still protecting my most important remaining gains on issueA and issueC. The opponent has mostly held firm at all valueE, with only a brief signal of flexibility on issueD to valueD, so I am matching that signaled concession and avoiding repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.38 by matching the opponent’s repeated valueE demands on every issue except issueA, where keeping valueB preserves meaningful utility on my most important issue. The opponent has been essentially holding firm at all valueE with no persuasive messages, so at t=0.52 I make a substantial mid-game concession while not yet dropping to their full bid.

I am targeting a mid-utility concession bid around 0.38 while still preserving value on one important issue. The opponent has been extremely firm on valueE across nearly all issues, so at t=0.54 I am matching valueE on four issues and holding only issueC at my best value to keep the offer above reservation while testing whether they prefer movement on issueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.36 by keeping issueC at my top value while conceding the other issues close to the opponent’s repeated pattern. At t=0.56 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent has held almost entirely at valueE with a recent signal for issueD: valueD, so I match that on low-priority areas while preserving value on issueC.

I targeted a raw weighted utility of about 0.274, conceding further because we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has been almost completely rigid around valueE on every issue, with recent signals that issueD: valueD may also be acceptable, and there are no natural-language messages to weigh. This bid matches their last offer on every issue except issueC, where I keep a compromise value rather than fully surrendering my remaining high-value issue.

At t=0.60 the opponent has barely conceded, but their latest offer improved issueC from valueE to valueD while keeping most other issues at valueE. Given the time pressure and their firm pattern, I am targeting a low but positive utility bid that closely matches what they have signaled they may accept, while taking the better issueD valueE from their latest offer.
